description: Configure Jest + CDK assertions for fine-grained and snapshot tests, fix the existing placeholder test to be a proper CDK stack assertion test, and ensure the test infrastructure is working correctly.
steps: Update jest.config.js to add snapshotSerializers if needed and ensure configuration supports both fine-grained assertions and snapshot tests with ts-jest. Remove the setupFilesAfterEnv entry for 'aws-cdk-lib/testhelpers/jest-autoclean' if it causes issues (verify first).

Rewrite test/cdk-base.test.ts to be an actual working test that imports the stack, creates it, and uses Template.fromStack() from 'aws-cdk-lib/assertions' to verify the stack synthesizes correctly (even if empty). Add both a fine-grained assertion test and a snapshot test.
Verify that 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx jest' passes with the updated tests.
Verify that 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx cdk synth' succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: jest.config.js is properly configured for CDK assertion tests
test/cdk-base.test.ts contains working tests using Template.fromStack() from aws-cdk-lib/assertions
At least one fine-grained assertion test and one snapshot test exist
Running 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx jest' passes all tests
Running 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx cdk synth' produces a CloudFormation template
findings: jest-autoclean setupFilesAfterEnv works fine with Jest 30 - no need to remove it. Empty CdkBaseStack does not produce a Resources key in the template when synthesized via Template.fromStack() (only Parameters and Rules). CDK Metadata is not added in test context. ts-jest warns about isolatedModules with NodeNext module kind but tests still pass.
